_meta.notes: First town east of Caelum on the way to Saltgale. Built on slightly higher mudflat — high enough to stay dry at the spring tides, low enough to feel the brine. Famous for its bioluminescent salt-flies, which lay eggs in the mud and glow blue at low tide. The flies are harvested at dawn and dusk for elven 'mudglass-prism' production (the glow becomes a marker dye in the firing).

tagline: Mudflats that glow blue at low tide. Don't step in them barefoot.
ambientPool: The mud at low tide flickers with a soft blue light, scattered like stars seen from below.
A flycatcher with a lacquered net moves slowly through a pool, gathering eggs without breaking the surface.
An elven Salt-folk wholesaler arrives in a small barge to negotiate a shipment of unfired mud.
The smell is iodine and something almost sweet.

rooms.summary: Wooden plank-walk at the edge of the mud. Walking on the mud directly is forbidden (you would crush eggs); the planks are the only way to reach the flycatchers' stations.
ambientPool: A flycatcher signals a shift change with one raised hand.
A glow-fly takes off from a pool, traces a silent blue line across the dark mud, lands again.
The tide is coming back. The eggs will be safe in twenty minutes.
An apprentice flycatcher learns the angle of the net by watching, watching, watching.

exits.narrative: Into the wooden shed where the eggs are weighed and stored.

rooms.summary: A long low-roofed shed at the high-water line. Eggs are weighed in tiny brass scales, stored in damp clay jars, sold by the half-handful to the Saltgale wholesalers.
ambientPool: A flycatcher pours eggs from a leather pouch into a brass pan.
An elven Salt-folk wholesaler watches the scale and takes one slow breath.
A child of the village sleeps in a corner — they were up since before dawn.
A glow-fly that escaped a pouch lights the back wall faintly.
